entry.title: torgo papers 2012-2016
entry.content:
Comparing Humans and Automatic Speech Recognition Systems in Recognition Dysarthric Speech

Data Description

The speaker-independent acoustic model are built using a subset of the TORGO database consisting of over 8400 utterances.

The SI models are trained and evaluated using the leave-one-out method. The held-out data from the test speakers is divided into an evaluation-set and an adaptation-set.

eight words: 4 phonetically similar words, 3 foils generated from the N-best list obtained from an HMM-based SR trained on the entire data.

Acoustic Features

Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs)

Linear Predictive Coding-Cepstral Coefficients (LPCCs)

Perceptual Linear Prediction (PLP) coefficients with various feature parameters, including the use of Cepstral Mean Subtraction (CMS), the use of the 0th order cepstral coefficient as the energy term instead of the log of the signal energy.

The recognition performance of the baseline of SI monophone models based on MFCC, PLP coefficients with the 0th order cepstral coefficient and LPCC are 39.94%, 39.5% and 34.33%, respectively.

comparison on speakers-adapted systems showed that PLP-based models outperformed MFCC-based systems by 2.5% absolute.

39 dimensions; 15ms for dysarthric speech; 10ms for non-dysarthric speech.

SI Baseline Models

40 left-to-right, 3-state monophone HMM and one single-state short pause model.

16 Gaussian mixture components per state.

eight-word finite-state grammar (为了对应之前的人类测试，从8个选项中选择一个正确的).

CMU pronunciation dictionary.

the baseline SI monophone models using PLP acoustic features on single-word recognition where eight alternatives are provided for each utterance is 39.5%.

Acoustic and Lexical Model Adaptation

analyze the pronunciation deviations of each dysarthric subject from the canonical form and build an associated speaker-specific pronunciation lexicon that incorporates their particular behavior of pronunciation.

Using the adaptation data from a particular speaker, we perform a two-pass MLLR adaptation: First, a global adaptation; Second, a regression class tree. This process resulted in 25.81% absolute (43.07% relative) improvement.

Using SD pronunciation lexicons, during recognition improved the word recognition rate further by an average of 3.18% absolute (8.64% relative)

In total, improved by 28.99% absolute (47.94% relative) yielding a mean word recognition accuracy of 68.39%; for non-dysarthric speech, the mean word recognition accuracy of the SI models is 71.13%. After acoustic model adaptation, the mean word recognition accuracy rise to 88.55%.

entry.title: 激活函数(ReLU,Swish,Maxout)
entry.content:
ReLU

ReLU(Rectified Linear Unit，修正线性单元)
$$
f(x)=\left{\begin{aligned}0,\ \ x≤0\x,\ \ x>0\end{aligned}\right.
$$
ReLU公式近似推导：
$$
\begin{aligned}f(x)=\sum_{i=1}^{inf}\sigma(x-i+0.5)\ \ \ \ (stepped\ sigmoid)
\ ≈log(1+e^x)\ \ \ \ (softplus\ function)
\ ≈max(0, x+N(0,1))\ \ \ \ (ReL\ function)
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
其中\sigma(z)=\frac{1}{1+e^{-z}}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ (sigmoid)
$$

softplus函数与ReLU函数接近，但比较平滑，同ReLU一样是单边抑制，有宽广的接受域(0, +inf)。

Noisy ReLU：ReLU可以被扩展为包括高斯噪音：
$$
f(x)=max(0, x+Y),Y∼N(0,\sigma(x))
$$
ReLU上界设置：
$$
f(x)=min(6,max(0,x))
$$
ReLU函数的单侧抑制、相对宽阔的兴奋边界和稀疏激活性使得它的表现很不错。

ReLU函数的缺点：

  1. ReLU强制的稀疏处理会减少模型的有效容量
  2. ReLU在x<0时梯度为0，这样就导致负的梯度在这个ReLU被置零，这个神经元有可能再也不会被任何数据激活，称为神经元“坏死”.
  3. 没有负值

关于稀疏性的观点：

信息解离

深度学习的一个明确目标是从数据变量中解离出关键因子。原始数据（以自然数据为主）中通常缠绕着高度密集的特征。原因是这些特征向量是相互关联的。如果能够解开特征间缠绕的复杂关系，转换为稀疏特征，那么特征就有了鲁棒性（去掉了无关噪音）。

线性可分性

稀疏特征有更大可能线性可分，或者对非线性映射机制有更小的依赖

稠密分布但是稀疏

稠密缠绕分布着的特征是信息最富集的特征，从潜在性角度，往往比局部少数点携带的特征成倍的有效。

而稀疏特征，正是从稠密缠绕区解离出来的，潜在价值巨大。

ReLU变种

Leaky ReLU

$$
f(x)=max(\alpha{x},x)
$$

其中α非常小，这样可以避免在x<0时，不能够学习的情况。

将α作为可以学习的参数，成为Parametric Rectifier（PReLU）

将α从高斯分布中随机产生时称为Random Rectifier（RReLU）

当α固定为0.01时称为Leaky ReLU

优点：

  • 不会过拟合
  • 计算简单有效
  • 比sigmoid/tanh收敛快

指数线性单元ELU

$$
f(x)=\left{\begin{aligned}\alpha(e^x-1), \ \ x≤0
\ x, \ \ \ x > 0 \end{aligned}\right.
$$

$$
f’(x)=\left{\begin{aligned}f(x)+\alpha, \ \ x≤0
\ 1, \ \ \ x > 0 \end{aligned}\right.
$$

有较高的噪声鲁棒性，同时能够使得神经元的平均激活均值趋近0；但是计算量较大

SELU

$$
f(x)=\lambda\left{\begin{aligned}\alpha(e^x-1), \ \ x≤0
\ x, \ \ \ x > 0 \end{aligned}\right.
$$

Swish

$$
f(x)=x·sigmoid(βx)
$$

β是个常数或可训练的参数，Swish具备无上界有下届、平滑、非单调的特性

当β=0时，Swish变成线性函数
$$
f(x)=\frac{x}{2}
$$
当β趋近无穷时，x>0，sigmoid取1，x<0，sigmoid取0，此时Swish变成ReLU
$$
f(x)=max(0,x)
$$
所以Swish函数可以看作是介于线性函数与ReLU函数之间的平滑函数

GELU

高斯误差线性单元是一个非初等函数形式的激活函数。

网络正则化方面，dropout将神经元输出随机置0，Zoneout将RNN的单元随机跳过。两者均是输出乘上服从伯努利分布的变量m~Bernoulli(p)，p为取正的概率。

希望p能够随着输入x的不同而不同，在x较小时以较大的概率将其置为0.由于神经元的输入通常服从正态分布，尤其是在加入了Batch Normalization的网络中，因此令p等于正态分布的累计分布函数即可满足。

正态分布的概率密度函数：
$$
f(x)=\frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{(x-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}}
$$
累积分布函数：
$$
F(x)=\frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}exp(-\frac{(t-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2})dt
$$
假设输入服从标准正态分布：X~N(0,1)，标准正态分布的累积分布函数习惯上记为Φ，Φ(x)=P(X≤x)

由于激活函数在训练和测试时使用方式完全相同，所以需要有确定性的输出（测试时需要）。

由于概率分布的数学期望是确定值，因此改为求输出的期望：
$$
E[mx]=xE[m]=xΦ(x)
$$
即对输入乘上伯努利分布的期望值p=Φ(x)
$$
Φ(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}exp(-\frac{t^2}{2})dt
\ = \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{0}^{x}exp(-\frac{t^2}{2})dt
\ = \frac{1}{2}(1+\frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{0}^{x}exp((-\frac{t}{\sqrt2})^2)\frac{dt}{\sqrt2})
\ = \frac{1}{2}(1+\frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{0}^{\frac{x}{\sqrt2}}exp(-z)^2dz)
\ = \frac{1}{2}(1+erf(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}))
$$
其中，erf称之为高斯误差函数，定义如下：
$$
erf(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{-x}^{x}exp(-{t^2})dt=\frac{2}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{0}^{x}exp(-{t^2})dt
$$
给出两个近似：
$$
xΦ(x)≈x\sigma(1.702x)
\ xΦ(x) ≈ \frac{1}{2}x[1+tanh(\sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi}}(x+0.44715x^3))]
$$

Maxout

Maxout可以看做是在深度学习网络中加入一层激活函数层，包含一个参数k。

其特殊之处在于增加了k个神经元，然后输出激活值最大的值。
$$
h_i(x)=\max_{j∈[1,k]}z_{ij},
\z_{ij}=x^TW_{…ij}, W∈R^{d×m×k}
$$

优点：

  1. Maxout的拟合能力非常强，可以拟合任意的凸函数
  2. Maxout具有ReLU的所有优点，线性、不饱和性，同时没有ReLU的一些缺点，比如神经元死亡

缺点：

  1. 整体参数的数量激增

Maxout激活函数

与常规激活函数不同，它是一个可学习的分段线性函数。

任何一个凸函数，都可以由线性分段函数进行逼近近似。如下图所示：

前两种函数就可以看作是两条直线的拼接
$$
f(x)=max(w_1^Tx+b_1,w_2^Tx+b_2)
$$

Sigmoid & tanh

sigmoid/logistic激活函数：
$$
\sigma(x)=\frac{1}{1+e^{-x}}
$$
tanh激活函数：
$$
tanh(x)=\frac{e^x-e^{-x}}{e^x+e^{-x}}
$$
tanh是对sigmoid的平移和缩放
$$
tanh(x)=2·\sigma(2x)-1
$$
导数：
$$
\sigma’(x)=\sigma(x)(1-\sigma(x))
\ tanh’(x)=1-tanh^2(x)
$$
sigmoid的优势：能够控制数值的幅度，在深层网络中可以保持数据幅度不会出现太大的变化

sigmoid的问题：

  1. 在离中心点较远的x处导数接近0，停止反向传播的学习过程
  2. sigmoid的输出不是以0为中心，而是0.5，这样在求权重w的梯度时，梯度总是正或者负的
  3. 指数计算耗时

为什么tanh比sigmoid收敛更快：

  1. 梯度消失问题，tanh的梯度消失问题比sigmoid要轻，因此收敛更快
  2. 以零为中心的影响：如果当前参数(w0,w1)的最佳优化方向是(+d0, -d1),则根据反向传播计算公式,我们希望 x0 和 x1 符号相反。但是如果上一级神经元采用 Sigmoid 函数作为激活函数，sigmoid不以0为中心，输出值恒为正，那么我们无法进行最快的参数更新，而是走 Z 字形逼近最优解。

激活函数的作用

  1. 加入非线性因素

  2. 充分组合特征。一般函数都可以通过泰勒展开式来近似计算，如sigmoid激活函数的指数项可以通过如下的泰勒展开式来近似计算
    $$
    e^z=1+\frac{1}{1!}z+\frac{1}{2!}z^2+\frac{1}{3!}z^3+o(z^3)
    $$
    其中有平方项，立方项及更高项，而z=wx+b，因此可以看做是输入特征x的组合。

参考文献

[1] https://www.cnblogs.com/neopenx/p/4453161.html

[2] https://www.cnblogs.com/makefile/p/activation-function.html

entry.title: 语言模型相关文件和Transition模型
entry.content:
发音词典与音素集

​ 用于整理发音词典的脚本如下：

​ 共包含如下五个步骤：

  • 下载CMU发音词典，去掉注释文本和多发音的标记。

  • 根据定义的词表，统计CMU词典中没有的词条，然后使用Sequitur工具生成这些词的发音

  • 将生成的发音与CMU中已有的发音结合，输出到发音词典文件中。检查汇总的发音词典中词条数目与词汇表中的数目是否一致。

  • 生成nonsilence_phones.txt文件（包含所有非静音的音素，每行对应一个基本音素）、extra_questions.txt（给出了构建音素的声学上下文决策树时会遇到的基本问题，每行对应一个聚类问题）、optional_silence.txt（定义了用于填充词间静音的音素，如SIL）和silence_phones.txt文件（定义了所有可以用来表示无效语言内容的音素，如SIL和SPN）

  • 生成lexicon.txt文件，包含了前面统计的发音词典的全部内容，并汇入了上述关于无效语音的发音规则。

​ 对于多发音的情况，Kaldi支持定义发音概率，对不同的发音加以区分，比如

 这种带发音概率的发音词典文件名为lexiconp.txt，当lexiconp.txt和lexicon.txt同时存在时，将使用lexiconp.txt。

​ 可以使用以下脚本检查发音字典文件夹中的文件内容和格式是否符合要求

语言文件夹

​ Kaldi示例通常将语言文件夹命名为data/lang。

  • phones.txt和words.txt这两个文件，分别定义了音素索引和词索引。示例内容如下：

$ head data/lang_nosp/words.txt
0 // 空符号
!SIL 1
2
3
…
#0 200004 // 消歧符号
200005 // 句首符号
 200006 // 句尾符号

Transition模型

​ 上面的示例<Topology>是由2个<TopologyEntry>构成，与语言文件夹中的topo文件内容是完全一样的。

​ 在Transition模型中，每个状态绑定了一个PdfClass。一般来说PdfClass值和HMM状态号相同。但如果有两个状态有相同的PdfClass值，则这两个状态将共享相同的概率分布函数。

​ <Triples>由众多三元组构成，每个三元组的定义为<音素索引，HMM状态索引，PDF索引>。把这些三元组全部放在一起，从1开始编号，每个编号对应一个transition state，这些三元组的个数就是transition state的个数。

​ transition state由若干可能的跳转指向其他状态，对这些状态从0开始编号，这样就得到了transition-index。

​ 把(transition-state, transition-index)作为一个二元组并从1开始编号，该编号就被称为transition-id。

​ 由这几个概念的定义可推知，transition-id可以映射到唯一的transition state，而transition state又可以映射到唯一的pdf-id，因此transition-id可以映射到唯一的pdf-id。

​ <LogProbs>中存储的是以e为底的转移概率。这个向量按transition-id索引，由于transition-id从1开始，所以需要在向量最前面加一个0

​ pdf-id不能唯一地映射成音素，而transition-id可以。使用pdf-id构建状态图，固然可以正常解码得到pdf-id序列作为状态级解码结果，但难以从解码结果中得到音素级别的识别结果。

参考文献

陈果果,都家宇,那兴宇,张俊博 .Kaldi语音识别实战[M].电子工业出版社. 2020.4

entry.title: 三音子模型训练
entry.content:
三音子的聚类裁剪

​ 将所有的三音子模型放到一起进行相似性聚类，发音相似的三音子被聚类到同一个模型，共享参数，通过人为控制聚类算法最终的类个数，可以有效地减少整个系统中实际地模型个数，同时又兼顾解决了单音子假设无效的问题。

​ Kaldi的具体实现是通过决策树算法，将所有需要建模的三音子的HMM状态放到决策树的根节点中，作为基类。接下来依照一套半自动生成的因素问题集合，对决策树中各个节点的三音子模型的中心音素、上下文音素以及HMM状态进行查询，按照最大似然准则优先进行节点分裂，每次分裂意味着一个新类的生成。通过控制似然阈值进而控制整棵决策树最终的叶子节点个数。

Kaldi中的三音子模型训练流程

​ 三音子模型的训练脚本功能由train_deltas.sh完成，其调用方法如下：

​ 叶子节点状态数的设定值与实际产生的状态数会有一定偏差，因为决策树分裂的内部实现原理是通过调整决策树分裂获得的似然增量阈值来停止分裂的，该机制无法准确控制叶子节点的数量，且最后叶子节点间还会进行一轮横向的二次聚类，该聚类会将叶子节点数进一步减少。一般来说最终产生的节点数为num-leaves设定值的80~90%。

​ acc-tree-stats和sum-tree-stats命令用于读取特征文件及其对应的对齐信息，计算决策树聚类过程中需要的一些统计量。

​ cluster-phones用于将单因素进行相似性聚类，生成了一套音素集合，然后用complie-questions来构造问题集。例如：

​ 该文件中的数字代表音素的id，其中每一行代表一个音素集。给定任何一个音素我们都可以对其提问“该音素是否落在这个集内”，因此我们可以将这音素集称为一个问题，整个文件就是一个问题集，供后面决策树分裂聚类使用。

​ 当然，Kaldi也支持加入人工编辑的问题文件。

​ 有了问题集，我们就可以用build tree命令来构建决策树了。Kaldi中的决策树的构建和模型参数绑定，是基于HMM状态级别的。我们可以用{L}-{C}+{R}.{S}来描述系统中的所有状态。

​ 最开始所有的状态{L}-{C}+{R}.{S}都被放置于根节点，被视为同一类。接着Kaldi中实现了两种节点的分裂方式：TE（Table Event）和SE（Split Event）。

​ TE指的是按列表的方式进行分裂。举例来说，我们对根节点的{S}进行TE，{S}={1,2,3}则根节点会分裂出三个子节点，{L}-{C}+{R}.1，{L}-{C}+{R}.2和{L}-{C}+{R}.3。类似的，对中心音素{C}进行TE，我们可以得到{L}-a+{R}.{S}，{L}-zh+{R}.{S}和{L}-g+{R}.{S}等。

​ SE指的是按问题进行分裂。面对一个树节点，一次分裂包含两个要素：问题的对象和问题的选择。问题的对象是指针对L、C、R、S的哪一个要素进行提问；问题的选择是指如何整个问题集中挑出“最好”的问题，Kaldi在实现中依靠最大似然准则来选取最优问题。

​ tree文件中的CE指的是叶子节点，后面跟的数字即pdf-id。在GMM-HMM模型中每个pdf-id对应着一个GMM模型；在DNN-HMM模型中，每个pdf-id对应神经网络的一个输出节点。

​ 接下来的工作是用convert-ali命令将对齐进行转换。需要转换的原因是每次进行三音子训练，都会重新生成tree文件以达到最合理的聚类绑定效果。因此逻辑模型到物理模型的映射关系会发生变化，需要将原有对齐中的相关信息进行转换，以保证与新tree文件一致。之所以进行多次三音子训练，是因为渐进式的模型迭代更稳妥，比如10000状态的三音子模型不是直接从10000个单音子模型的对齐训练，而是先训练5000个状态的三音子模型，再用5000个状态的三音子模型对训练数据进行对齐，然后用新的对齐再去训练一个10000状态的三音子模型。

​ 接下来就是模型训练流程，通过指定轮数的EM算法迭代，按照固定间隔穿插维特比重对齐和GMM模型的高斯混合分量分裂。

一些内部实现

EventType

在Kaldi中用EventType数据结构来表示三音素

vector中包含四个数对，分别表示三个音素和状态，其中音素用(位置,音素id)来表示，状态用(-1,状态)来表示，其中位置和状态都从0开始编号。

举个例子，假设我们当前的三音素为a-b+c.2，其中a,b,c音素的id分别为10，11，12，则可以表示为：

Clusterable和GuassClusterable

Clusterable是一个纯虚类，作为Kaldi聚类机制的统一接口。在三音素决策树状态绑定这一块，我们主要用到的是继承自该基类的GuassClusterable。

Clusterable对象的主要作用是把统计量累加在一起和计算目标函数。

在forced alignment之后，从左到右扫描对齐数据，我们能从中得到三音素及HMM状态和对应的特征向量，也就是一个EventType和其对应的特征向量。在扫描过所有训练数据后，出现的每个EventType会对应多个特征向量。

目标函数，即L(S):
$$
L(S)=-\frac{1}{2}(log[(2\pi)^2|\sum(S)|]+n)\sum_{s∈S}\sum_{f∈F}\gamma_{s}(o_f)
$$
其中γ表示后验概率，Σ表示协方差。

对角协方差的对角线上是特征向量集每一维的方差，D(X)=E(X^2)-(E(X))^2，因此需要知道特征向量集的和以及特征向量集的平方和；同时维特比训练对齐之后，同时状态集S的后验概率之和我们可以用对应的特征向量出现的次数来代替。

因此与一个EventType相关的统计量就应该包括对应的特征向量个数、特征向量的累加、特征向量的平方的累加。这三个值就是GaussClusterable中需要保存的值。如果把多个EventType的统计量累加在一起，就可以计算这些EventType组成的状态集的似然，因为一个EventType实际就是一个state。

BuildTreeStatsType

AccumulateTreeStats()

主要功能：这个函数拿到一句话的特征序列和对齐序列后，从对齐序列(transition-id序列)能够得到对应的音素序列和对应的HMM状态id，这样就得到了一个个EventType。每个transition-id对应的特征向量就是属于该EventType的数据，用该EventType对应的GaussClusterable对象累计相关的统计量。

对所有的特征数据、对齐数据执行这个函数后，我们就从训练数据里得到了所有EventType和该对象对应的统计量。这些统计量可以被用于自动产生问题集和构建决策树。

cluster-phones

  • 作用：对多个音素或多个音素集进行聚类
  • 输入：决策树相关统计量treeacc，多个音素集sets.int
  • 输出：自动生成的问题集（当mode为questions调用AutomaticallyObtainQuestions()函数得到问题集）

AutomaticallyObtainQuestions()

它把音素集聚类成一棵树，并且对树中的每一个节点，把从该节点可以到达的所有叶子节点结合在一起构成一个问题。

  • 读取sets.int中所有的音素保存到phones变量中

  • 调用FilterStatsByKey()把stats中属于三音素第二个HMM状态的统计量留下（具体第几个，由all_pdf_class指定，而为什么是HMM状态是因为key是kPdfClass=-1）

  • 调用SplitStatsByKey()根据三音素的中间音素（由参数P指定，这里P=1）对retained_stats进行划分，把属于每个音素的统计量放在一个BuildTreeStatsType中，得到split_stats

  • 调用SumStatsVec把split_stats每个元素中的所有统计量加起来，得到每个中间音素的统计量，也就是summed_stats，其维度为音素个数

  • 根据sets.int指定的集合，累加同一个集合中音素的统计量，最终的summed_stat_per_set的维数就是sets.int的行数

  • 调用**TreeCluster()**，对summed_stat_per_set进行聚类，生成相关信息。

  • 调用ObtainSetsOfPhones()生成问题集。先解释下该函数的几个参数：

    • phone_sets：由sets.int生成，每个元素代表sets.int中一行上的音素集
    • assignments: phone_sets中每个元素所属的cluster（叶子节点）
    • clust_assignments：上一步生成的数的每个节点的父节点
    • num_leaves：叶子节点个数
    • question_out：生成的问题集

    函数内容：

    • 得到每个叶子节点的音素集
    • 将子节点的音素集加入到其父节点的音素集中
    • 把原始的phone_sets插入到问题集中（行号变成音素集）
    • 过滤问题集的重复项、空项，生成最终的问题集

TreeCluster()

该函数首先初始化一个TreeClusterer对象，把统计量points传给对象；然后调用该对象的Cluster方法获取关于聚类结果的相关信息。

TreeClusterer对象和Node数据结构

Node节点：

  1. 保存着指向双亲结点和孩子节点的指针，其中children是一个Node指针的vector
  2. 保存着属于该节点的所有统计量之和node_total
  3. 保存着是否是叶子节点，以及是叶子节点时在leaf_nodes_中的索引和不是叶子节点时nonleaf_nodes_中的索引
  4. 如果是叶子节点，保存着属于该叶子节点的那些点的统计量，以及这些点在所有点组成的vector中的索引，即points_中的索引。用best_split保存着对该叶子节点进行最优划分时，获得的最大似然提升。assignments中就保存着对该叶子节点进行最优划分后，该叶子节点中的点被划分到哪一个簇

TreeClusterer：

  1. leaf_nodes_保存着全部的叶子节点，nonleaf_nodes_保存着全部的非叶子节点
  2. points_中保存着每个点的统计量。该对象的聚类过程就是为了把这些点分成一簇簇(cluster)
  3. queue_是一个优先队列，队列中每个元素是一个对，这个对的第二个元素是节点指针，第一个元素是对该节点进行划分获得的最大似然提升

TreeClusterer对象初始化

调用init()完成初始化工作。init()生成树的根节点top_node，根节点包括传递给该对象的所有点points_，初始化根节点的信息，包括is_leaf=true、index=0、parent=NULL、node_total和leaf中的成员，并把根top_node放进leaf_nodes_中（注意末尾带_的是TreeClusterer的数据成员，不带_的是node的数据成员）

每当创建新结点的时候，应该总是调用FindBestSplit(Node* node)函数。该函数调用ClusterKMeans()找到属于该node的点的最优划分和对应的似然提升，并把似然提升记录到leaf.best_split中。如果超过cfg_指定的似然阈值thresh，则把对(best_split, node)放进优先队列queue_中。

TreeClusterer.Cluster()

该成员方法根据统计量构造树，实现对点的聚类，将点分成一簇簇，该树的一个叶子节点代表一个簇，一个簇都包含几个点，一个点就是sets.int中的一行，即一个音素集。

对于建好的树，从一个节点都可以访问到一个（该节点就是叶子节点）或多个叶子节点，把这些叶子节点中的点合在一起，就构成了一个更大的音素集，也就构成了一个“问题“。

  1. 构建树。对当前树的每个叶子节点都可以进行划分（刚开始只有根节点）。queue_队首就是对当前所有叶子节点进行划分后似然提升最大的一个叶子节点，调用TreeClusterer.DoSplit(Node* node)对该节点进行划分，重复对队首划分，直到queue_为空。

    DoSplit(Node* node)对node执行具体的划分，生成新的两个孩子节点，把node的index给第一个孩子，并把leaf_nodes_该index对应的node替换成第一个孩子，为第二个孩子生成新的index并加入到leaf_nodes_，根据划分时计算的assignments，把node的点划分到两个子孩子中；把node加入到nonleaf_nodes_；最后对两个孩子节点分别调用FindBestSplit()，得到两个孩子节点的最优划分，更新queue_。

  2. 生成ObtainSetsOfPhones()要用到的信息。

    • 调用CreateAssignmentsOutput(assignments_out)，计算传递给TreeClusterer的每个点分别属于哪个叶子节点。把这些点属于的叶子节点编号写到assignments_out中
    • 调用CreateClustAssignmentsOutput(clust_assignments_out)，计算树的每个节点的双亲节点的编号。因为叶子节点和非叶子节点分开编号，为了避免重复需要重新编号：先对非叶子节点进行编号，然后对叶子节点进行编号

EventMap

几个int32的别名:

  • EventKeyType
  • EventValueType
  • EventAnswerType：表示发射概率密度函数(p.d.f)的编号pdf-id。每个HMM状态都有一个pdf，所以每个EventType都对应一个pdf-id。状态绑定想做的事就是使多个EventType对应到同一个pdf-id，这样就能减少参数。

EventMap就是对EventType到pdf-id映射关系的定义，具体来讲，EventMap对象的成员方法**Map()**实现了从EventType到EventAnswerType的映射。

ConstantEventMap

ConstantEventMap表示决策树的叶子节点。叶子节点只需要保存一个EventAnswerType类型的变量answer_，用来保存该叶子节点对应的pdf-id即可。

SplitEventMap

SplitEventMap表示决策树的非叶子节点。

对一个给定EventType，在其决策树的每一个非叶子节点，我们都会问一个问题以决定进入该非叶子节点的哪一个分支，比如“左边的音素属于问题集1吗？”，“右边的音素属于问题集20吗？”，那我们该如何表示左右呢？

可以用EventKeyType类型的变量来表示位置信息，定义为key_。

同样地，我们可以用EventValueType类型的变量来表示音素，用vector<EventValueType>类型的变量来表示音素集，定义为yes_set_.

此时我们对每个非叶子节点的问题等价于“第key_个位置的音素属于音素集yes_set_吗？”

最后我们用EventMap *来表示指向孩子节点yes_和no_的指针

TableEventMap

一般来说我们有n个不同的音素（集），每个音素3个状态就需要建立3n个决策树，Kaldi想把3\n个决策树放到一棵大树里，这棵大树有3*n个叶子节点。为了提升划分效率，相比于SplitEventMap一次只能生成2个叶子节点，TableEventMap可以生成多个叶子节点。

TableEventMap的数据成员包括进行划分的位置EventKeyType key_，以及指向对其划分后所有子节点的指针向量std::vector<EventMap*>> table_.

Map()

  • SplitEventMap::Map()，我们首先查找EventType第key_个位置的值value是什么，若该value在yes_set_中，则递归调用yes_孩子的Map()；否则递归调用no_孩子的Map()。直到孩子节点是一个叶子节点ConstantEventMap，直接返回保存pdf-id的EventAnswerType answer_.
  • ConstantEventMap::Map()：直接将ans设置为answer_
  • TableEventMap::Map()：在构建扩展之前的决策树，TableEventMap也可以作为非叶子节点。该Map()方法会检查EventType第key_个位置的值tmp是否在table_范围内，如果在并且table_[tmp]不为空，就对该table_[tmp]递归调用Map()，直到到达叶子节点CE，返回answer_

roots文件

roots文件指明在决策树的聚类过程中，哪些音素应该共享树根。对放在roots文件同一行的音素共享一个树根。

同时roots文件通过”shared”或者”not-shared”来说明一个音素的不同HMM状态是否应该共享一个决策树根，当然即使不同状态共享树根也可以通过对状态提问，即kPdfClass(-1)位置的值来让不同状态的HMM状态落入不同的叶子节点中。

roots文件还通过”split”或者”not-split”说明了对当前决策树的树根是否应该进行划分。

build-tree

  • 作用：构建决策树

  • 输入：累积的统计量treeacc、问题集questions.qst、roots.int、HMM拓扑topo

  • 输出：决策树tree

  • 过程：

    1. 读取roots.int，得到vector<vector<int> > phone_sets，vector<bool> is_shared_not 和 vector<bool> is_split_root
    2. 读取topo文件，得到保存HMM拓扑结构的对象HmmTopology topo
    3. 读取treeacc，得到累积的统计量BuildTreeStatsType stats
    4. 读取questions.qst，得到Questions qo
    5. 调用topo.GetPhoneToNumPdfClasses(&phone2num_pdf_classes)得到vector<int> phone2num_pdf_classes，其元素保存每个音素对应的HMM状态数
    6. 调用**BuildTree()**，返回保存大决策树的EventMap *to_pdf
    7. 用to_pdf初始化对象ContextDependency ctx_dep(N, P, to_pdf)，并将ctx_dep写到文件tree。Kaldi用类ContextDependency对决策树进行封装，其数据成员包含音素宽度N_(=3)、中间音素位置P_(=1)、决策树to_pdf_

    BuildTree()

  1. 调用GetStubMap()得到初始的决策树EventMap *tree_stub，也就是扩展前的决策树
  2. 调用FilterStatsByKey()，只留下roots.int中指定为split的音素的统计量
  3. 调用SplitDecisionTree()，对tree_stub进行扩展，生成整个大的决策树EventMap *tree_split：把tree_stub的每个叶子节点扩展成决策树。
  4. 调用ClusterEventMapRestrictedByMap()，对tree_split每个音素的决策树上的某些叶子节点进行合并，得到EventMap *tree_clustered
  5. 调用RenumberEventMap()对tree_clustered的叶子节点重新编号（从0开始），得到EventMap *tree_renumbered.

GetStubMap()

GetStubMap()的作用就是构建决策树D，使其每个叶子节点都对应roots.int中的一行的决策树的树根

函数的核心代码可以分成三块，分别是创建叶子节点CE、非叶子节点TE和非叶子节点SE。

首先来看非叶子节点SE。首先我们将phone_sets等分成两份，然后对这两部分递归调用GetStubMap()，得到两个子树map1和map2，然后创建一个非叶子节点SE，使其key_为P(=1)，其yes_set_是phones_sets的前一半，yes_指向map1，no_指向map2。

一直向下调用时我们就会遇到phones_sets只包含一个元素，因此我们就会进入创建叶子节点CE的条件分支，如果每一行都指定shared，那么就设该叶子节点的answer_为num_leaves_out，即在roots.int中的行号-1。

当某一次调用GetStubMap()时发现phone_sets的大小不为1，但是每一个元素只包含一个音素，此时我们就生成一个TE，令其key_为1。假设phone_sets有6个元素，这样就能生成6个叶子节点。

SplitDecisionTree()

我们要对tree_stub的每个叶子节点，也就是每个音素集构建一棵决策树，实现状态绑定，减少GMM参数。我们对决策树持续进行划分，直到总的叶子节点数达到max_leaves或者所有叶子节点的最优划分带来的最大似然提升都小于thresh。

首先调用SplitStatsByMap()，根据tree_stub对stats进行划分得到split_stats，把对应到同一叶子节点的统计量放在一起。对tree_stub的每一个叶子节点构建一个DecisionTreeSplitter对象，使用这个对象构建对应音素的决策树。初始化DecisionTreeSplitter（简称DTS）对象需要传入该音素集的统计量和全部的问题集。

在初始化DTS对象时，都会找到这个DTS对应的叶子节点的最优划分(DTS.FindBestSplit())：首先对于EventType的每一个key，在该key对应的每个问题集中（由q_opts给出）找到一个问题，使得划分后的似然提升最大；然后比较这四个似然提升，找到最大的提升，将DTS的key_设置成似然提升最大的key，DTS的yes_set_设置为该key_上取得最大似然提升的问题，指针yes和\no分别指向两棵子树。

把所有的决策树放在一起比较，每次对似然提升最大的叶子节点进行划分（builders[i]->DoSplit()），这种策略通过优先队列实现。

对tree_stub的每个叶子节点，调用builders[i]->GetMap()得到由EventMap表示的属于该音素的决策树，这就是input_map.Copy(sub_trees)完成的工作。

参考文献

[1].陈果果,都家宇,那兴宇,张俊博 .Kaldi语音识别实战[M].电子工业出版社. 2020.4

[2].https://download.csdn.net/detail/u010731824/9807460

entry.title: 美丽中国纪录片摘录
entry.content:
Part3

Hidden deep in the mountains of western China’s Sichuan province, there is a primitive (原始的) and dreamy valley (山谷) called Jiuzhaigou.

It has preserved one of the most stunning view of nature that it could be called China’s Garden of Eden. Its signature landscape is the crystal lakes with turquoise (深蓝色) and many other colours of white, blue, green, or emerald (翠绿色).

The secret of colouring the lake is the spirogyra (水绵), charophyte (轮藻), and other kinds of ferns (小蕨) that grew in the water. They contain chlorophyll (叶绿素), which helps create various colors. These colors vary according to the season, light and the viewing angle.

There are 108 lakes like this dotted(点缀在) around the three major valleys in Jiuzhaigou. Each of these valleys has an altitude higher than 2000 meters and altogether the valleys are 49 kilometers long.

Besides these magical lakes, Jiuzhaigou also has China’s widest waterfall, Nuorilang Waterfall, with a width of 270 meters.

If you visit Jiuzhaigou during the right time, you may even experience the rotation of four seasons in one day. From the colourful scene to the white appearance, Jiuzhaigou could do it in very short time. It is perhaps the ultimate fairyland (桃花源) in China.

part4

The Earth is a place of endless wonder where landscapes are often created from the mere dust. The Loess Plateau (黄土高原) is such a place.

It is located in the north of central China, and contains 70% of the loess on the planet. It is hard to image a time when it did not exist. But this yellow land was created by particles carried by the wind. The entire mega (巨大的) landscape has a total area of 620,000 square kilometers. Even seen from above, it’s hard to comprehend the sheer (完全的) size of it.

The loess dune (沙丘) is dozens (几十) of meters high, and 300 meters at its thickest.

Eight million years ago, super strong winds blew sand and dust here. The entire process of dust carrying and stacking took more than 2.6 million years. Scientists can tell the wind direction by the thickness of the loess. Because tiny dust particles are carried the furthest, they came to rest in the southeastern most part of the plateau (高原). The sand in the northwest part of the plateau is much rougher.

Where the river crossed, an alluvial (淤积的) plain was deposited (淤积). People gradually started to settle around these fertile (肥沃的) areas. They farmed in the valley, and built roads between the ravines (沟壑). Finally, they made this ancient land their home and prospered (繁荣) generation and generation.

Part 5

Starting from Hainan Island, the southernmost province of China, you can reach Sansha City at the southern tip by this transportation supply ship. Sansha is made up of more than 280 islands, sandbars, reefs (珊瑚礁) and other sea areas.

April to May are the most beautiful seasons in this part of China. The sea is colorful with an average visibility of more than 30 meters. Looking down from a high altitude, the islands in front of us do not seem to be connected with each other, but they are known as the Seven Connected Islands. That’s because beneath the sea, there is a huge reef holding them together, forming a common foundation (基座) for the little islands. As the tide rises and falls, they display a variety of features.

Most of the islets (岛屿) here are made up of coral (珊瑚虫) remains. Coral secretes (分泌) limestone (石灰石), which becomes their living shells. They gather together and grow and multiply from generation to generation. Layer upon layer of limestone is added and then compacted to form the coral reef we see today. This reef is still growing, towards the sea at a rate of about 2.5 centimeters per year.

Part 6

Daocheng Yading is a magical and mysterious land located in the southwest of China’s Sichuan province. This is a kingdom surrounded by breath-taking (叹为观止) views of snow-capped mountains, steep glaciers (冰川), crystal clear lakes, vast pastures (草地), as well as dense woods. It is called the last pure land on this blue planet.

Every September, the scenery here is at its most fascinating. It’s like placing people into a colorful fairy tale world (童话世界). The focal point (焦点) is the three mountain peaks in Daocheng Yading. The most beautiful is called Yang Maiyong (央迈勇). It is about 6,000 meters in height, and has the shape of a perfect pyramid.

Back in 1931, the American explorer Joseph Rock first introduced it to the world with photos taken for the National Geographic magazine. He wrote, “she is the most beautiful snow peak my eyes have ever seen”. Next to it, stand two other mountains: Xiannairi (仙乃日) and Xianuoduoji (夏诺多吉). The crest (顶峰) of the mountains is covered by snow most of the time. No one has yet ever been able to reach the top. They are called the “Three Holy Mountains”. It is an irreplaceably sacred (神圣的) place in the hearts of the local people.

The local say if one could walk round the holy mountains three times, then your wishes for this life will be fulfilled. But it is such a great challenge to both one’s physical ability and mind because of its altitude. What drives them to do that? Perhaps it is simple faith. It is not only the last pure land but also earth that can purify the soul.

Part 7

There is a rare spectacle (壮观) to be found at the end of the glaciers beneath the main peak of a mountain area in southwestern China. The surface of the rocks here is an eye catching bright red.

This is the heart of the Hengduan Mountains, a mountainous region in southwestern China. 145 snowy peaks over 5,000 meters above sea level are clustered here, forming an amazing group of mountains. The highest peak, Gongga Mountain, is 7,556 meters above sea level. It’s the highest point in the mountainous area of Southwest China. Its giant conical (锥形) peak is the cradle (发源地) of glacier formation, from which 74 mature glaciers extend in all directions.

The Hailuogou Glacier in the east has a huge icy waterfall measuring 1,100 meters wide, with a drop of more than 1,000 meters. The strong thawing (融冻) effect leads to frequent massive icy avalanches (冰崩). Giant glaciers descend all the way into dense forests. With its tip, extending to 2,850 meters above sea level, it is the lowest maritime (海洋性) glacier in Asia.

Bright red rocks can be seen almost everywhere in the valley at the end of the glacier. Scientists have found that the red blood color on the stone is not from a mineral, but a rare parasite (寄生物), a rare lichen (地衣) complex. Whenever the temperature and humidity are right, they spread across the rock surface dyeing (染色) the rocks bright red. Taken away from the mountains however, they quickly change color and die. Even they are left in peace, their life span is only 4-5 years, and then they will regenerate and begin dyeing the rocks bright red again.

Why they came into being has baffled (困惑) many great minds.

Part 8

In China’s southeast coastal (沿海) province of Zhejiang, there is a group of giant rocks called the Sanpan Stone, literally meaning, the three pieces of stone. Once upon a time (从前) however, they were one whole piece of rock.

The story of the Sanpan Stone started 135 million years ago. Back then, during a violent tectonic (造山) event, powerful forces formed a basin (盆地) covering nearly 347 square miles. Imagine the basin as a cradle (摇篮) in which the Sanpan Stone is nestling (发育). In this huge bowl, sedimentary (沉积的) rocks gathered, dominated by conglomerates (砾岩). These sediments were the origins of the Sanpan Stone. The process of mountain building continued. The sediments in the basin were forced up to form a sedimentary massif (山体), which gradually grew into the Sanpan Stone.

For tens of millions of years, wind and water eroded the massif along vertical fractures (切割) until most of the rock was weathered away (雨蚀风化). Eventually, the three individual rocks with steep (陡峭) cliffs we see today were formed. The highest of them measures 324 meters. However, the steep cliffs pose a great challenge for those wishing to reach the top.

About 500 years ago, in China’s Ming Dynasty a group of carpenters (木匠) was hired to build a wood ladder in order to reach the top of the Sanpan Stone. They toiled (持续) for three long years, but failed in their task. Before the ladder could reach the summit, its foot had rotted away (腐烂). The ambition of reach the top was not achieved until the end of 1980s. Over 3,500 stone steps were carved (开凿) into the rock face .Now, we can finally climb these steep stairs and reach the top, to experience the power of the tectonic movements from the remote past.

part 9

Wuyi Mountain, located in the southeastern part of China, has a total area of about 1,000 square kilometers. It has the largest nature reserve in Fujian Province, and largest existing subtropical (亚热带) primary forest system in the same latitude zone in the world.

Wuyi Mountain is deeply influenced by a fault zone (断裂带) which controls the direction of the ridge line (山脊线). The other faults divide the mountain into several fault blocks and form many deep valleys.

The Tongmuguan (桐木关) Fault Zone is the center of the faults, forming a spectacular “V”-shaped grand canyon (峡谷). The intense (剧烈的) tectonic movements of millions of years ago gifted Wuyi Mountain a magnificent Danxia landform which differs from the Danxia landform in Northwest China. Many of the rock surfaces are covered with different shades of vegetation creating a blush (腮红) on the huge green mountains.

The Goddess Peak, the symbol of Wuyi Mountain, is the typical residual Danxia landform left by the faults. Geological (地质学的) movement caused a red-rock massif to collapse (倒坍) under its enormous (巨大的) weight. The Goddess Peak is the last remaining pillar (柱峰) of the surrounding rocks after the collapse.

There are many cracks in the karst rocks (赤石岩) of Wuyi Mountain. After millions of years of scouring (冲刷) the cutting by water flowing through them, many streams have been formed around the mountain.

Sitting on bamboo rafts (竹筏) and following the stream, tourists can enjoy the scenery of Wuyi Mountain along the way.

part 10

In Northwestern China, there is a chain of undulating (起伏) hills called Colorful Zhangye (张掖) Danxia. Here you can find the colors of the rainbow.

The terrain (地带) is so hostile (不利) that even grass struggles to survive. So where did this palette (七彩的) of colors come from? It’s hard to imagine, but this area was once a lake. The multi-colored hills are the result of the long geological evolution of the lakebed. Changes in temperature and humidity, as well as the existence of various mineral elements, and the different iron content in lake sediments especially, gave rise to the spectrum (韵律) of colors.

Part of the ancient Silk Road (丝绸之路), it is said that Marco Polo once visited here over 700 years ago. However, compared to Marco Polo, today we can have much more fun.

Part 11

There are no less than 30 lakes named “West Lake” in China. But the West Lake in Hangzhou is the best known of them all. It is located in Hangzhou, the capital of Zhejiang Province on the southeastern coast. And the city is known as the Paradise of the World in China.

Thousands of years ago, the West Lake was connected to the sea. The Qiantang River, not far away from the West Lake, enters the sea in the eastern part of this area. Sediment deposited (沉积) by the river eventually blocked the connection between the West Lake and the sea, making it an inland water feature.

Since the 9th century A.D., the beautiful scenery of the West Lake in all seasons has appeared time and again in poems, stories and legends. It’s roughly estimated there are about 630 legends of the West Lake alone.

In 2011, West Lake Cultural Landscape of Hangzhou was included on the World Heritage List. It is visited by an endless stream of international tourists. Perhaps some of them will add their own stories to West Lake’s wealth of literature.

Part 12

The Giant panda, you might think it’s still an endangered species. But the good news is, populations are on the rise, due to enormous efforts by Chinese conservation programs, using new breeding technology by actively guiding adult pandas to mate, more and more panda babies are being born. But today there is an even bigger challenge, some of the panda cubs (幼崽) from these breeding programs are being reintroduced to the wild, their native home. The first thing they have to learn, is how to climb trees.

A panda cub is having a lesson in the forest. He is already one and a half years old. That’s his mother next to him, a wild giant panda. Mother and son are taken to a safe wild habitat and are set free to make their own way. The ability of young cubs to survive in the wild is mainly taught by their mothers. The most important skill is to climb trees. Because in this forest, they may encounter enimies, such as other bears.

The little panda seems to have no interest in practicing this new skill. But mother is strict, she won’t allow her son to be distracted (开小差). This is about life and death. Finally our little panda climbs up to the top of a tree. See how sharp his claws (爪子) are, and the strength in his limbs. The most important thing he has acquired is great courage, a natural instinct he inherits from his wild panda mother. Now it’s time to rest, after a tiring day. Who’s a big strong boy then?

Part 14

In China, the red-crowned crane (丹顶鹤) is regarded as a very special animal. According to zoologists, individual wild cranes can live to be 20 to 30 years of age. For the Chinese, it is often associated with luck, fidelity (忠诚) and long life.

In eastern China’s coastal city of Yancheng, red-crowned cranes arrive each year for the winter. Among China’s extensive large bird population, it’s known for its elegance. This graceful (又没得) creature has white feathers, a red crown on its head and a beautiful and elegant posture.

Red-crowned cranes are monogamous (一夫一妻). They have strict needs when it comes to their living environment. They will only breed where the natural habitat is friendly. Compared with their breeding grounds thousands of miles away to the north, the water here never completely freezes over in winter. It’s full of food, and they can hide among the reeds. But this longevity (长寿) bird so cherished (珍视) by the Chinese people is endangered, because its natural habitat is fast disappearing with only about 2000 left in the world. 1000 of them migrate to Yancheng and overwinter each year in the vast wetlands here. This is the largest group of red-crowned cranes in the world that continues to migrate.

Thanks to local efforts in wetland protection and scientific measures to better look after this national treasure (国宝), the number of the red-crowned cranes is now rising. The yancheng wetland has become a paradise for red-crowned cranes ensuring the longevity birds enjoy a long life on this planet.

Part 15

On the coastal wetlands by the Yellow Sea in eastern China, you’ll notice a strange-looking deer walking around. It has a head shaped like a horse, giant antlers (角) like a deer, hooves like a cow and the tail of a donkey. These are Milu deer, a species native to China. But it disappeared from China, for nearly 100 years. The fact that they have now returned to China is a result of a remarkable century-old animal protection collaboration between two countries.

Archaeological (考古) studies have found that the Milu deer were once common in eastern China, but by the nineteenth century, their numbers had dropped drastically. Most of the Milu deer were raised in the Emperor’s hunting grounds outside Beijing. The Europeans who came to China at the time believed the species to be very rare and many were taken back to Europe. When the Emperor’s hunting grounds were destroyed by a heavy flooding. The Milu deer were lost never to be seen in the area again. Luckily, there were the Milu deer that had been shipped to foreign countries.

A Duke (公爵) in Britain had given shelter to 18 of these fortunate Milu deer, and they were well taken care of. By the mid-1980s, the Chinses government hoped the species could be restored in China once again, a suggestion supported by the Duke’s descendants (后代). In 1986, 39 of the Milu deer were flown to China from Britain to their ancestral home in Dafeng County in Jiangsu province. After two decades, the Milu deer population in China has increased to over 5000 with nearly 1350 roaming (漫步) in the wild. Today, there is no longer any fears for their survival in their homeland

Part 16

The Qiantang River is located in Zhejiang on the southeastern coast of China and flows into the East China Sea. Every year on the eighteenth day of the eight lunar (月) month, people gather along its banks (畔) expectantly, eager for the arrival of the tide.

The waters of Hangzhou Bay (杭州湾) and the Qiantang River meet on the horizon (地平线), forming a thin white line. Hundreds of thousands of tons of water per second the fusion (融合) of ocean and river dramatically tumble (跌落) back towards the land. The seawall contains the force of 7 tons per square meter. The waves can reach a maximum of 9 meters about three stories high and even taller than four of China’s famous basketball player, Yao Ming.

The huge tides and waves do little to deter the bold (勇士). People jumped into the powerful waves as early as the Southern Song Dynasty of China more than 800 years ago. The people of the time called them the Tide Players. It looks so much like today’s surfing, minus the surf board.

Today, the Qiantang River attracts thousands of people from far and wide for the annual tidal surfing competition.

Part 17

In winter, Arxan (阿尔山), a city in northern China, becomes a world of ice. Its rivers become frozen all except one.

Upstream of the Khalkhyn Gol River (哈拉哈河), there is a stretch (段) around 20 kilometers long. Even in winter when the air temperature typically drops to minus 40 to 30 degrees celsius, this section remains ice free. People call it, the Unfrozen River.

Its secret lies deep beneath the river bed where the river passes over an area of geothermal (火山) activity near Arxan. The heat from below raise the temperature allowing the river water to stay above freezing point and so it remains ice free.

During the harsh winter, it even becomes a spa for animals. A group of cattle are strolling along the river bank. They can’t wait to wade (淌) through the water. Look closely and you’ll see steam rising from the surface of the river. Such a nice warm bath! Let’s leave the cattle to enjoy it.

Part 18

In southern China, there is a famous road built across Poyang Lake, China’s largest freshwater lake. The people of China call “the most beautiful road under the water”.

Towards the end of May every year, this road begins to attract a lot of attention. The color of the lake either side begins to change into two different tones. The surface of the road is so closed to the water level of the lake. As you drive along the view ahead is broad and bright. It feels as though you are riding a boat across the lake.

People call it the road under the water. For the next few months, this road is no longer just above the surface but deep under the water. As the water level of Poyang Lake rises, the road is gradually flooded. Although drivers can no longer see the road surface, they can still drive across safely by keeping within the guard rails on the either side. For thrill seeking motorists (刺激), it’s like driving an amphibious (水陆两栖) supercar. As the water levels continue to rise, after a few days, the entire road will be completely submerged (掩藏). Eventually, a few months later, the road reappears again

Part 19

During winter in China’s Heilongjiang Province, it is not uncommon to see temperatures below minus 30 degrees Clesius. Despite the intense cold, ‘flowers’ still find a way to bloom. In the right place with the right conditions, these flowers spread across the tops of the trees.

Every year, Kurbin (库尔滨) enjoys a “flower season” of up to four months. This amazing ice flower spectacle can be experienced from December to March each year. Ice flowers are officially known as “rime” (雾凇). In extremely cold weather, the nearby hydropower station (水电站) continuously discharges (排出) hot water. The water vapor (水蒸气) evaporates (蒸发) from the surface of the water, forming a dreamy drifting (漂) white mist. Must of the water vapor turns into ice at night as temperature drop which then settles on the tree branches creating this fantastical sight.

As you walk beneath the trees covered with rime, sometimes you can hear the sound of ice crystals growing and then falling. The rime absorbs all the sounds around, creating a tranquil (安静的) fairy tale world.
